skillName: Area of rectangles, parallelograms, triangles and compound shapes
misconceptionSummary: Students use the slant side instead of the perpendicular height in the triangle and parallelogram area formulas.

steps.narration: Rectangle area is length times width. Eight centimetres by five centimetres is forty square centimetres.
Parallelogram area uses the perpendicular height — the vertical distance between the parallel sides — not the slant side. Base nine times height four is thirty-six square centimetres.

lines.text: Triangle: Area = ½ × base × perpendicular height
Base 10 cm, height 6 cm → ½ × 10 × 6 = 30 cm²

steps.narration: A triangle is half a parallelogram. Area equals one half times base times perpendicular height. Ten times six times one half is thirty square centimetres.

visuals.expression: Rectangle: l×w.  Parallelogram: b×h.  Triangle: ½×b×h.
visuals.label: Always use perpendicular height — never the slant side

steps.narration: Three formulas, one rule: always use the perpendicular height. The slant side is for perimeter only.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: using the slant side in the parallelogram or triangle area formula. Area requires the perpendicular height — the right-angle distance between the base and opposite side.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Area always uses perpendicular height — the vertical drop from base to opposite side. The slant side is longer and gives an incorrect, too-large area.
